prompt: What AI tools and infrastructure are startups and small AI teams actually PAYING for in 2026 (not free or open-source)? Where is real, proven willingness-to-pay? Cite sources.
content:
**LLM APIs (especially Anthropic’s Claude/Claude Code), AI coding tools like Cursor and GitHub Copilot, specialized GPU/cloud compute providers like CoreWeave and Together AI, and productivity tools like Notion AI and Perplexity Pro stand out as areas with clear, proven paid adoption by startups and small teams in 2026.**[[1]](https://www.salesforce.com/artificial-intelligence/ai-for-small-business/best-ai-tools/startups/)[[1]](https://www.salesforce.com/artificial-intelligence/ai-for-small-business/best-ai-tools/startups/)

These are not free/open-source options; they feature paid tiers, per-seat pricing, API usage, or compute contracts that teams actively subscribe to or scale up on. Evidence comes from adoption surveys, high company revenues/valuations, real-world spending examples (e.g., enterprises and teams hitting usage caps), and infrastructure backlogs.

### LLM APIs and Agentic/Coding Tools
Startups and small engineering teams heavily pay for frontier model access via APIs or integrated coding environments, particularly for development velocity, research, and agentic workflows. 

- **Claude (Anthropic) and Claude Code**: Frequently cited for startups due to strong context windows, safety features, and agentic coding capabilities. One 2026 analysis noted ~75% adoption among startups (under 50 people) for agentic/multi-step coding work. Real spending signals include Uber exhausting its full 2026 AI budget in ~4 months (by April) after widespread rollout of Claude Code to engineers, leading to internal caps (e.g., $1,500/month per employee/agentic tool).[[2]](https://www.forbes.com/sites/janakirammsv/2026/05/17/uber-burns-its-2026-ai-budget-in-four-months-on-claude-code/)[[3]](https://www.ideaplan.io/blog/ai-coding-assistant-market-share-2026)
- **GitHub Copilot**: Paid tiers with broad IDE integration; high workplace adoption (e.g., ~56% in larger enterprises, strong among developers overall). It remains a default for many due to Microsoft distribution and reliability.[[3]](https://www.ideaplan.io/blog/ai-coding-assistant-market-share-2026)
- **Cursor (Anysphere)**: AI-native code editor popular with startups and AI-first/small teams for advanced features like multi-file editing, agents, and higher capability. It has seen rapid revenue growth (reports of doubling to ~$2B ARR range in early 2026 periods) and strong uptake among power users/startups seeking leverage beyond basic autocomplete.[[4]](https://tech-insider.org/github-copilot-vs-cursor-2026-2/)

These tools show willingness-to-pay through direct usage (token/compute costs or subscriptions) and high CSAT/NPS in developer surveys, with 84%+ of developers using AI coding tools daily in some 2026 data.[[5]](https://ai.plainenglish.io/claude-code-vs-cursor-vs-github-copilot-in-april-2026-i-tested-all-three-on-production-systems-fed398b02a5e)

### AI Infrastructure and Compute
For teams training, fine-tuning, or running inference at scale (beyond free tiers or local setups), specialized cloud/GPU providers see paid commitments:

- **CoreWeave**: GPU-native cloud optimized for AI workloads (training/inference). It serves AI labs, startups, and enterprises with flexible scaling; major contracts (e.g., with OpenAI, Meta) demonstrate demand, and it has expanded support for smaller innovators via funds/hubs. Pay-for-capacity or usage models apply.[[6]](https://www.siliconflow.com/articles/en/the-best-ai-infrastructure-2026)[[7]](https://sacra.com/c/coreweave/)
- **Together AI**: Focuses on serverless inference and developer platforms with pay-per-token pricing (e.g., starting low per million tokens for various models). Positioned for startups/small teams deploying open or proprietary models without managing hardware.[[8]](https://www.respan.ai/market-map/compare/coreweave-vs-together-ai)

Broader context: AI infrastructure spending dominates overall forecasts (e.g., Gartner projections of trillions in total AI spend by 2026, with heavy infra weighting), and providers like these have massive backlogs and funding tied to customer demand.[[9]](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/2/19/visualising-ai-spending-how-does-it-compare-with-historys-mega-projects)

### Productivity, Research, and Operations Tools
Small teams pay per seat or via subscriptions for integrated AI features that boost daily output:

- **Notion AI**: Add-on pricing (~$10/user/month) for knowledge management, search, summarization, and drafting within workspaces.[[1]](https://www.salesforce.com/artificial-intelligence/ai-for-small-business/best-ai-tools/startups/)
- **Perplexity Pro**: Paid per-seat access for cited, real-time research and analysis as a search/research replacement.[[1]](https://www.salesforce.com/artificial-intelligence/ai-for-small-business/best-ai-tools/startups/)
- **Salesforce Starter Suite** (with AI agents): Paid tiers for CRM/operations, unifying data and providing AI insights for lean teams.[[1]](https://www.salesforce.com/artificial-intelligence/ai-for-small-business/best-ai-tools/startups/)

Other mentions in startup tool roundups include tools like Gamma for design/content generation (paid tiers with proven revenue, e.g., crossing significant ARR with small teams).[[10]](https://www.forbes.com/lists/ai50/)

### Evidence of Real Willingness-to-Pay
- **Spending behavior**: Uber’s budget overrun on Claude Code; internal caps at companies signal high but managed usage. Overall AI spend projections (e.g., $2T+ globally in 2026 per Gartner) reflect enterprise and startup investment in these layers.[[2]](https://www.forbes.com/sites/janakirammsv/2026/05/17/uber-burns-its-2026-ai-budget-in-four-months-on-claude-code/)[[9]](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/2/19/visualising-ai-spending-how-does-it-compare-with-historys-mega-projects)
- **Adoption and revenue data**: High developer usage rates; rapid ARR growth for tools like Cursor; infrastructure providers with multi-billion backlogs and customer commitments.[[4]](https://tech-insider.org/github-copilot-vs-cursor-2026-2/)
- **Startup-specific lists**: Curated recommendations (e.g., Salesforce’s 2026 guide) consistently highlight paid versions of the above as scalable for bootstrapped/funded teams, emphasizing time-to-value and predictable costs over free tiers.[[1]](https://www.salesforce.com/artificial-intelligence/ai-for-small-business/best-ai-tools/startups/)

**Caveats**: Small teams often start with usage-based pricing (APIs/tokens) to control costs before committing to seats or capacity. Preferences vary by need (e.g., coding vs. research vs. infra). Data draws from 2026 reports, surveys, and company disclosures; actual spend depends on scale and workload. Open-source alternatives exist but the paid options above demonstrate clear commercial traction where teams value reliability, speed, support, or advanced features enough to pay.
